title: Lists & keys
description: Rendering arrays and why keys matter.

options: Uniquely identify list items across renders for efficient diffing
questions.explanation: Without stable keys, React may unmount/remount items on reorder.

options: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop'
questions.explanation: Add key={x.id} or another stable identifier.

questions.prompt: Using array index as key is bad when:
options: The list can reorder, insert, or delete — state gets stuck on the wrong item
questions.explanation: Index is fine for static, append-only lists — otherwise use stable ids.
Siblings only. Different lists can reuse the same keys.

options: A regular prop the child receives
Consumed by React, NOT forwarded to the child
questions.explanation: If you need it, pass it under a different prop name.
